model: Stochastic Block Model with archetype-weighted edge probabilities
network_description: Sparse directed influence graph across the 551-agent QGIA population. Edge probabilities governed by a Stochastic Block Model partitioned by division membership, modulated by tier proximity, combined contrarian index, and geometric mean trust radius. Four edge types encode distinct social epistemic functions.

collaborate.description: Active analytical partnership within shared division; routine product exchange

challenge.description: High combined contrarian index; dissent relationship — source challenges target assessments

reinforce.description: Shared archetype + similar prior strength; mutual confirmation relationship

inform.description: Cross-division information flow; source provides domain product to target

network_statistics.structural_note: Challenge edges (7.9%) are deliberately sparse — this is a feature, not a bug. In a 551-person analytical organization, genuine contrarian relationships are rare. The 583 challenge edges represent the analyst pairs whose dissent will actually be heard. Reinforce edges (4.7%) flag echo-chamber risk clusters for monitoring.

usage_notes.scenario_runs: When running a live scenario (e.g. Iran escalation), load the subgraph of analysts assigned to that scenario's Crisis Response Cell. Edge types determine whose dissent propagates. Challenge edges create the analytical tension; reinforce clusters are the groupthink risk.
usage_notes.echo_chamber_detection: Identify connected components of reinforce edges with 3+ nodes. These are the clusters most likely to produce overconfident consensus product.
usage_notes.dissent_propagation: High-contrarian-index analysts connected via challenge edges to Tier 3+ nodes have outsized influence on final product. Monitor these paths.

edges: [FULL EDGE ARRAY — 7407 edges. See session compute artifact code_file:222 for complete edge list. Structure per edge: {source: agent_id, target: agent_id, type: string, weight: float}]
